title: centaurium tenuiflorum subsp. viridense
intro: Centaurium tenuiflorum subsp. viridense, also known as grass-of-parnassus, is a plant species that belongs to the gentian family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its content of iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Centaurium tenuiflorum subsp. viridense include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. Centaurium tenuiflorum subsp. viridense can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
